records.ety: [F. idiotisme, L. idiotismus the way of fashion of a private person, the common or vulgar manner of speaking, Gr. <?/, fr. <?/ to put into or use common language, fr. <?/. See Idiot.]

defs: An idiom; a form, mode of expression, or signification, peculiar to a language.
quotes: Scholars sometimes give terminations and idiotisms, suitable to their native language, unto words newly invented.

defs: Lack of knowledge or mental capacity; idiocy; foolishness.
quotes: Worse than mere ignorance or idiotism.
The running that adventure is the greatist idiotism.
